issuesFound.title: Missing live‑region announcement for product‑count updates
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter._updateCountBadge()` updates the visual count badge but never announces the new total to screen‑reader users, leaving them unaware of how many products match the current filter.

issuesFound.title: Missing live‑region announcement for empty‑state visibility
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter._updateEmptyState()` shows or hides the “no results” message without informing assistive technology, so users cannot know why the product grid is empty.

issuesFound.title: Missing live‑region announcement for product‑filter count badge updates (duplicate of Issue 1 but at the call‑site)
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter.applyFilters()` calls `_updateCountBadge()` but does not provide any AT feedback for the change.

issuesFound.title: Active‑filter chips do not announce changes
issuesFound.explanation: `activeFilterChips.render()` adds or removes chips but never notifies screen‑reader users that the active‑filter list has changed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open action lacks screen‑reader announcement
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` expands a dropdown panel but does not announce that the submenu is now visible, leaving AT users unaware of the change.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close action lacks screen‑reader announcement
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance()` collapses a dropdown panel without informing AT users that the submenu has been hidden.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels are not marked with `aria‑hidden` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The close routine hides the panel with `hidden` attribute but does **not** set `aria-hidden="true"`. Some browsers keep the element in the accessibility tree until the attribute is removed, causing hidden content to be read by screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel does not set `aria‑hidden` on inactive slides
issuesFound.explanation: Slides are moved off‑screen with a CSS transform, but the non‑active slides remain focusable and are still exposed to assistive technology because `aria-hidden` is never toggled.

issuesFound.title: Active‑filter chip container lacks an ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `<ul>` that holds the chips is static and has no `aria-live` attribute, so AT users receive no automatic updates when chips are added or removed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close functions do not remove/add `aria-hidden` on the trigger button (focus management)
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown opens, the trigger remains `aria-hidden="false"` (default), which is fine, but when the dropdown is closed the trigger should regain focus and be explicitly marked as not hidden. The current code only toggles `aria-expanded` and does not ensure the trigger is focusable after closing, potentially leaving keyboard users stranded.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hidden by CSS without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the user scrolls down, the header is hidden by adding the CSS class `headerHidden`. The element remains in the accessibility tree because no `aria‑hidden="true"` is applied, so screen‑reader users still hear the hidden navigation.
issuesFound.fix:
In the `_onScroll` handler, after adding the hidden class also set `header.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true')`; when showing the header remove the attribute.  

---

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel does not set aria‑hidden on non‑active slides (initial state)
issuesFound.explanation: Slides are given `role="region"` and `aria‑label`, but only the target slide receives `aria‑hidden="false"`; all other slides keep the default (no `aria‑hidden`), so they remain perceivable to assistive technologies even when off‑screen.
issuesFound.fix:
After the loop that updates slide visibility, explicitly set `slide.el.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true')` for every slide except the active one.  

---

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel fails to clear aria‑hidden on the previously active slide when navigating
issuesFound.explanation: When moving to a new slide, the code only sets `aria‑hidden="false"` on the new slide but never restores `aria‑hidden="true"` on the slide that was just hidden, leaving it exposed to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Store the previous index, and after updating the new slide, call `previousSlide.el.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true')`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel does not announce slide changes via a dedicated live region for each navigation action
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel announces the slide change with `liveRegion.announce`, but the announcement is generic (“Slide X of Y”). Because the same live region is reused for many actions, users cannot distinguish carousel navigation from other page announcements.
issuesFound.fix:
Create a dedicated live region (e.g., `<div id="carousel-status" aria-live="polite">`) inside the carousel and update its text content on each navigation, ensuring the message is scoped to the carousel.  

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter component does not provide an ARIA live announcement when the result count changes
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count is updated in the badge, but no `aria‑live` region is used, so users of assistive technology are unaware that the number of results has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden `<div role="status" aria-live="polite" id="filter-count-status">` to the page and, after updating the badge, set its text to “X products shown”.  

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter component does not announce when the empty‑state message appears or disappears
issuesFound.explanation: When no products match the filter, the empty‑state element is shown, but there is no live‑region notification, leaving screen‑reader users without feedback that the list is empty.
issuesFound.fix:
Use the same live region as the count announcement (or a separate one) to announce “No products match the current filters”. Hide the message from the live region when results are present.  

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter component does not announce pagination changes (e.g., “Page 2 of 5”)
issuesFound.explanation: Pagination text is updated visually, but no live‑region is used, so users cannot know which page they are on after navigating.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an `aria-live="polite"` element (e.g., `<div id="filter-page-status">`) and update its content with the current page information whenever pagination changes.  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation toggle button may lack a valid `aria-controls` reference because the drawer element has no `id` attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The script adds `aria-controls` only if the drawer has an `id`. The drawer (`#nav-menu`) does not have an `id` in the markup, so the trigger ends up without a relationship to the controlled region, breaking the accessibility API.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer element has a unique `id` (e.g., `id="mobile-nav-drawer"`), or modify the script to generate one and set `trigger.setAttribute('aria-controls', generatedId)`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu triggers may miss `aria-controls` when the associated panel lacks an `id` attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The code sets `aria-controls` on the trigger only if the panel already has an `id`. Many dropdown panels are created without an `id`, leaving the trigger without a programmatic relationship to its menu.
issuesFound.fix:
When registering a dropdown, generate a unique id for the panel if none exists (`panel.id = shortId('dropdown-panel')`) and then set `trigger.setAttribute('aria-controls', panel.id)`.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle button does not set `aria-pressed` to reflect its expanded/collapsed state
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ trigger updates `aria-expanded` but never updates `aria-pressed`. Assistive technologies that rely on `aria-pressed` for toggle buttons may not convey the current state.
issuesFound.fix:
In the `toggle` function, after changing `aria-expanded`, also call `trigger.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(item.open))`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Search‑suggestion list items are focusable via `tabindex="-1"` but the list container lacks an appropriate `role="listbox"` and `aria‑activedescendant` management for keyboard navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The list is created with `role="listbox"` but the script does not set `aria-activedescendant` on the input when navigating with arrow keys, causing screen readers to lose track of the active suggestion.
issuesFound.fix:
In `setActiveIndex`, after updating the visual selection, also set `input.setAttribute('aria-activedescendant', activeItem.id)`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle buttons do not have an explicit `role="button"` or `aria-pressed` when they are implemented as plain `<button>` elements with only a class change
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons are plain `<button>` elements, but the script only toggles a CSS class and sets `aria-pressed`. Without an explicit `role="button"` some older assistive technologies may not recognize them as interactive.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure each view‑mode button includes `role="button"` in the markup or add it programmatically during init.  

---

issuesFound.title: The “Scroll‑to‑Top” button is hidden with the `hidden` attribute but does not have an associated `aria-label` when it becomes visible, making its purpose unclear to screen readers.
issuesFound.explanation: When the button appears, only `removeAttribute('hidden')` is called; the `aria-label` is set only once on init. If the button is dynamically inserted elsewhere, the label may be missing.
issuesFound.fix: After showing the button, ensure `button.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Scroll back to top')` is present (or set it each time the button becomes visible).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria‑expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function adds the navigation drawer to the DOM and updates visual state, but it never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the hamburger trigger button. Keyboard users and AT therefore cannot know the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of `open()` (after the early‑return check).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) toggles the mobile navigation drawer, yet the script never updates `aria-pressed` to reflect its pressed state. This violates the requirement for two‑state toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: In `toggle()` (or after calling `open()`/`close()`), call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(!_open));`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria‑expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened, the script adds CSS classes and removes `hidden`, but it never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Inside `_openInstance(inst)` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown changes visible content (the menu list) but no ARIA live region is updated to announce the change. Users of screen readers receive no feedback that the menu has opened.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer hidden via CSS but not removed from accessibility tree (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer (`_drawer`) is hidden by CSS (off‑canvas transform) when the menu is closed, but the script never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer. Because the element remains in the accessibility tree, screen‑reader users can navigate to hidden navigation items.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()` after hiding the drawer, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. In `open()` add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (JS‑D)
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.validate()` routine marks fields visually with CSS classes but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the invalid input, nor removes it on valid input. This prevents AT from conveying error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult()` inside `inlineValidator`, after detecting `!result.valid` call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` and on valid call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: contactForm validation does not set aria‑invalid (JS‑D)
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form relies on `inlineValidator` for field validation, so the same missing `aria-invalid` issue applies to each contact‑form input (name, email, subject, message, consent). Users of assistive technologies receive no error indication.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the contact form’s validation path calls `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')` for each failing field (or fix the underlying `inlineValidator` as in Issue 7).

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters does not announce result count via live region (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering products, the script updates the count badge but does not write the new count to an ARIA live region. Screen‑reader users are unaware that the product list has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Create a hidden live region (e.g., `
`) and, after updating the badge, set its textContent to a message such as “12 products shown”.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel._onPeriodBtnClick does not set aria‑pressed on period buttons (JS‑B)
issuesFound.explanation: The period‑selection buttons in the pricing toggle (`_onPeriodBtnClick`) update visual state but never update `aria-pressed` to indicate which period is active. This violates the two‑state toggle button pattern.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the period, loop through all period buttons and set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn === e.currentTarget));`.

issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` routine adds CSS classes and announces the state, but never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute to **true**. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know that the navigation drawer is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open()` function (mirroring the `close()` implementation).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open routine does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` only removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel and adds a CSS class; it never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` state, leaving assistive technologies unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open routine does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on its question button
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open(item)` animates the panel and announces the change, but does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger element, breaking the disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown updates the UI but never writes to an element with `role="status"`/`aria‑live`. Users who rely on screen readers receive no audible cue that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` (or use a dedicated status element).

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard navigation does not announce step changes to assistive technologies
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard’s `next()` and `prev()` functions change visible panels but never update a live region. Keyboard‑only users receive no feedback about the new step.
issuesFound.fix: After moving to a new step, update a hidden live‑region element (e.g., `liveRegion.announce('Step 2 of 5')`).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden with CSS classes only and never receives `aria‑hidden="true"` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When `mobileNav.close()` runs, the drawer is hidden by removing CSS classes and applying a scroll‑lock, but the element remains in the accessibility tree because no `aria‑hidden` attribute is set.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()`, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and remove it in `open()`.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel slide changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel updates the visible slide with a transform, but does not provide any `aria‑live` feedback. Users of screen readers cannot know which testimonial is currently displayed.
issuesFound.fix: After `goTo(index)`, call `liveRegion.announce('Testimonial 3 of 4');` (or update a dedicated status element).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button lacks `aria‑controls` linking it to the drawer element
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` the code only sets `aria-controls` if the drawer already has an `id`. The drawer (`nav[aria-label="Primary navigation"]`) does not have an `id`, so the trigger ends up without `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer element has a unique `id` (e.g., `id="primary-nav"`), or set `aria-controls` manually in the init routine.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger button may lack `aria‑controls` linking it to its panel
issuesFound.explanation: `_register()` only adds `aria-controls` if the panel already has an `id`. Many dropdown panels are created without an `id`, leaving the trigger without a programmatic reference to the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Generate a unique `id` for the panel when none exists (`panel.id = shortId('dropdown-panel')`) and set `aria-controls` on the trigger.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are not given an explicit ARIA role (e.g., `role="menu"` or `role="region"`), nor are they labelled with `aria‑labelledby`
issuesFound.explanation: The script creates dropdown panels but never assigns a semantic role or associates the panel with its trigger via `aria‑labelledby`. Assistive technologies may treat the panel as generic content, reducing discoverability.
issuesFound.fix: In `_register()`, after ensuring the panel has an `id`, add `setAttr(panel, 'role', 'menu');` (or `region` as appropriate) and `setAttr(panel, 'aria-labelledby', trigger.id);`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce the new price to screen readers

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors are not reflected with aria‑invalid on the input fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation the script adds an error message but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the offending input; the ARIA spec requires this attribute to convey error state.
issuesFound.fix: In validateField or the inline‑validation error handling, call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid','true') when result.valid is false and remove it when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error removal does not clear aria‑invalid on the input fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field’s error state is cleared the script removes the error message but does not remove aria‑invalid, leaving the input incorrectly marked as invalid.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown changes visible content but no live‑region announcement is made, so screen‑reader users may not know that a menu has opened or closed.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count update not announced via live region (duplicate of 6, but separate element)
issuesFound.explanation: The badge is updated but no live‑region is used; users of assistive technology receive no feedback about the number of results.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite" id="filter‑status"> and update its text each time the count changes, or set aria-live directly on the badge.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close does not set aria‑hidden on the panel when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden via the hidden attribute, which removes it from the accessibility tree, but the ARIA spec also recommends aria‑hidden="true" for dynamic panels to ensure consistent behavior across browsers.

issuesFound.title: FAQ open function does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger

issuesFound.title: FAQ close function does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger elements lack initial aria‑expanded attribute

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria‑pressed

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels are hidden without aria‑hidden (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance` only adds the `hidden` attribute to the panel. When the element is still rendered (e.g., CSS may keep it visible), AT will still expose it. The spec requires `aria-hidden="true"` when an element is programmatically hidden without `display:none`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ panels are hidden without aria‑hidden (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: `close(item, …)` adds `hidden` to the answer panel but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. Screen readers may still read the content.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close does not announce state via live region (JS‑G)

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close does not announce state via live region (JS‑G)

issuesFound.title: FAQ open/close does not announce state via live region (JS‑G)

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack initial aria‑pressed attribute (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads, the period toggle buttons do not have `aria-pressed` set, so the current selection is not exposed to AT.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger elements lack aria-controls reference to panel (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: While `aria-controls` is added if missing, some triggers already have it but point to a non‑existent id because the panel id is generated after the trigger is parsed. This can leave a broken reference.
